forget_claims.C001: Yeast is a living single-celled microorganism.
forget_claims.C002: Yeast consumes sugars and produces carbon dioxide gas through fermentation.
forget_claims.C003: Carbon dioxide produced by yeast becomes trapped in dough, making bread rise.
forget_claims.C006: A developed gluten network traps gas bubbles, giving bread its structure.
forget_claims.C007: Proofing is the rest period during which yeast produces gas and the dough expands.
forget_claims.C016: The browning of a bread crust during baking is caused by the Maillard reaction.
